evidence_excerpt: Two years after diagnosis she was well during pregnancy, though she developed worsening cutaneous lesions around delivery; intestinal studies normalized on a gluten-free diet, indicating improvement in CD but ongoing VP-related skin issues without sustained overall deterioration.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Recurrent skin fragility/blistering with later worsening, scarring, and hyperpigmentation around delivery despite sun screens; CD-related intestinal function normalized on diet.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Intestinal permeability and absorption studies were normal two years following reinstitution of a gluten-free diet.
Two years after diagnosis, she developed worsening of the skin lesions, scarring, and hyperpigmentation one month prior to and immediately after delivery, despite sun screens.
Two years after diagnosis, during pregnancy at 17/40 weeks gestation, she was well.

notes_on_uncertainty: The case report describes two conditions (variegate porphyria and celiac disease) with limited long-term follow-up detail. Functional status is not explicitly quantified; classification as mild is inferred from the statement that she was well, with ongoing skin lesions. Deterioration of skin symptoms around delivery appears episodic rather than sustained.
